name: Western Asturian

ancestors.notes: Stronger Gallaecian Celtic substrate in the western areas, bordering Galician-speaking territory.
Suebi superstrate, stronger in western Asturias (Suebi kingdom centred in Gallaecia).

notes: Western Asturian (Asturianu occidental). Spoken in the western parishes of Asturias, bordering Galicia. KEY DIALECTAL FEATURES: (1) Latin F- aspiration: word-initial /f/ → [h] (e.g. 'filu' → [hiɾu]), written ⟨h⟩ in this dialect — shared with Western Galician, Gascon, and Medieval Castilian. (2) Che vaqueira: ⟨ḷḷ⟩ / ⟨l.l⟩ = a palato-alveolar affricate [tʃ] from Latin PL-, CL-, FL- (e.g. 'ḷḷover' < PLOVERE), a hallmark of the vaqueiros de alzada. (3) ⟨h⟩ = [h] (phonemic aspiration, unlike silent h of Central). (4) Some degree of l-frication absent in central varieties. Sources: García Arias (1974), Neira Martínez (1955), Canellada (1944).

sources.author: García Arias, X.Ll.
sources.title: Gramática histórica de la lengua asturiana

sources.author: Penny, R.
sources.title: A History of the Spanish Language
